pairs.question: When was Andrew Bridgen born and which constituency did he represent as a Member of Parliament?
pairs.answer: Andrew James Bridgen was born on 28 October 1964. He served as the Member of Parliament (MP) for North West Leicestershire from 2010 until 2024.

pairs.question: What political party did Andrew Bridgen belong to during most of his parliamentary career, and when was he expelled from it?
pairs.answer: Andrew Bridgen was a member of the Conservative Party for most of his parliamentary career. He was expelled from the party in April 2023 after criticizing COVID-19 vaccines and making controversial statements.

pairs.question: What were the reasons for Andrew Bridgen's suspension from the House of Commons in January 2023?
pairs.answer: Andrew Bridgen was suspended from the House of Commons for five days in January 2023 after the Commons Select Committee on Standards found that he had repeatedly breached rules regarding paid lobbying and declaring interests. Additionally, he attempted to pressure the commissioner investigating his lobbying breaches.

pairs.question: Which political party did Andrew Bridgen join after being expelled from the Conservative Party, and when did he resign from this party?
pairs.answer: After being expelled from the Conservative Party, Andrew Bridgen joined the Reclaim Party in May 2023. He resigned from the Reclaim Party in December 2023.

pairs.question: What was Andrew Bridgen's stance on Brexit, and which Conservative prime ministers did he support efforts to remove from office?
pairs.answer: Andrew Bridgen was a supporter of Brexit. During his parliamentary career, he supported efforts to remove Conservative prime ministers David Cameron, Theresa May, Boris Johnson, and Liz Truss from office.

pairs.question: What happened to Andrew Bridgen in the 2024 UK general election?
pairs.answer: In the 2024 United Kingdom general election, Andrew Bridgen ran as an independent candidate for the North West Leicestershire constituency but lost his seat and forfeited his deposit.

pairs.question: How did Andrew Bridgen's parliamentary career end after leaving the Reclaim Party?
pairs.answer: After leaving the Reclaim Party in December 2023, Andrew Bridgen sat as an independent MP for the remainder of the 2019 Parliament. He subsequently ran as an independent in the 2024 UK general election but was not re-elected.
